what_it_does: Acts as a post-compaction handoff log for the DNA/design-render lane, recording mission context, current state, pending work, verification rules, source-vs-render lessons, file locations, coordination channels, and cautionary operating constraints.
observations: Opens with an instruction to read DNA-REPO-FACTS first and frames this document as an opinion-laden working log rather than a factual record.
Names the session id and recall mechanisms, including a raw transcript path and company recall commands.
Defines the DNA lane mission as building a reusable ConceptV visual grammar across presentations, print, web/device app, interaction, onboarding, dashboards, and comparison surfaces.
Records a build-priority sequence covering tokens, artefact organisms, interaction states, blueprint systems, N-panel workflows, and computed responsive allocation.
Maintains detailed state for FACE-1 surfaces including session-card, decision-cards, channel-view, board-view, timeline lanes, transcript visualization, nav rail, and later surfaces.
Repeatedly distinguishes rendered output, fabric-complete work, Tim-verified loops, and final closure by human use.
Documents a correction from photo-vs-line-art framing to broader design dimensions: size, space, colour, shadow, position, difference, and diagram/symbol density.
Records a system-level direction to extract and apply design language from a dense source subset before scaling to the full source set.
Lists many concrete files, generated assets, ports, routes, commits, screenshots, and proof pieces relevant to the lane's work.
The provided file text ends with an explicit truncation note.
standouts: The document is both a transfer pack and a correction ledger, preserving prior mistakes alongside revised operating rules.
Its central acceptance rule is closed-loop verification through Tim's use, not render existence or self-assessment.
The current design direction is system-wide generative visual density extracted from source material, not isolated per-card fixes.
It encodes coordination boundaries between DNA, lead, composition, projection, recollection, and fork lanes.
conventions: Uses emphatic section markers, warnings, stars, all-caps labels, and inline status symbols to distinguish urgency and state.
Keeps superseded context in place while marking it with corrections, cautions, or current-direction headers.
Names commits, screenshots, ports, routes, and files inline as operational anchors.
Uses compact status notation such as DONE, PENDING, OPEN, VERIFIED, SHIPPED, and GATE to track work.
Separates stated lane ownership from other lanes' responsibilities, especially composition assets and projection hosting.
concerns: The file is very long and retains superseded directions, so readers must resolve current-vs-old sections carefully.
It mixes factual pointers, subjective critique, personal directives, runtime coordinates, and roadmap state in one document.
Several sections warn against trusting earlier appraisals, which makes unqualified status extraction risky.
The supplied content is truncated, so the final coordination section is incomplete from this view.
notes: If briefing someone on this file: treat it as a dense DNA-lane handoff with strong cautions about verification, current design direction, and pending surface work.
questions: From this file alone, which listed runtime hosts and proof pieces are currently active versus historical?
From this file alone, what exact schema from DESIGN-LANGUAGE-EXTRACT.md must be applied next?
From this file alone, what part of the truncated coordination section remains after the projection note?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it is a read-first post-compaction transfer pack, though it also functions as a roadmap, correction log, verification doctrine, and coordination ledger.
intention_signals: It appears intended to preserve working memory and prevent repeated mistakes by telling the next DNA-lane agent what to trust, what not to claim, what remains open, and how to continue the visual system work.
novelty: A handoff document that combines compaction recovery, design-system roadmap, live-surface status, critique doctrine, and inter-lane protocol in one operational ledger.
contribution: Contributes a file-local record of DNA-lane intent, verification constraints, visual-language extraction direction, surface render queue, known gaps, and coordination handoffs that a fused system would retain as context for design/render behavior and state recovery.
summary_for_embedding: Post-compaction DNA-lane transfer pack for counterpart-design documenting visual grammar mission, current render/surface state, verification rules, design-language correction, pending work, file map, and coordination context.
intention_for_embedding: The file was written to let a resumed agent continue the DNA/design-render lane without repeating prior appraisal errors or losing the current source-driven generative design direction.
